expected_decisions.summary: Adopted Auth0 as the authentication provider, rejecting in-house development and Clerk.
expected_decisions.verbatim_quote: Sara: Good. Decision: we go with Auth0 for authentication. Marc owns the integration.

expected_decisions.summary: Adopted per-seat pricing at launch with three tiers (Starter, Growth, Enterprise), deferring usage-based pricing to Q2.
expected_decisions.verbatim_quote: Sara: Three tiers. Starter, Growth, Enterprise. Decision: per-seat pricing for launch, three tiers, usage-based deferred to Q2.

expected_decisions.summary: Metering infrastructure work deprioritized and deferred until Q2 usage-based pricing is formally committed.
expected_decisions.verbatim_quote: Sara: Yes. Don't build it until we commit to Q2 usage-based.

expected_decisions.summary: Selected Postgres on RDS with multi-AZ failover, automated backups, and pgvector extension available on demand.
expected_decisions.verbatim_quote: Marc: Good. Decision: Postgres on RDS, multi-AZ, automated backups, pgvector available when we need it.

expected_decisions.summary: Decided against running a separate analytics database for at least one year; all workloads stay on the primary Postgres instance.
expected_decisions.verbatim_quote: Marc: One more thing — we are not running a separate analytics database for at least the next year. Everything runs on the same Postgres until we have a real reason to split.
